description: Fix extra leading space in dev mode log output for tsc and any other child processes that emit lines with leading whitespace or ANSI escape sequences.

userStories.title: Strip leading whitespace and ANSI clear-screen escapes from piped child process output
userStories.description: As a developer running npm run dev, I want all prefixed log lines to be consistently aligned without extra leading spaces, so that the terminal output looks clean and uniform.
acceptanceCriteria: The pipeWithPrefix function in scripts/dev.ts trims leading whitespace from each line before writing it (using line.trimStart() or equivalent)
The pipeTscStdout function in scripts/dev.ts trims leading whitespace from each line before writing it
ANSI clear-screen escape sequences (\x1Bc, \x1B[2J, \x1B[H) emitted by tsc --watch are stripped from line content before prefixing
The sentinel detection (line.includes('Watching for file changes')) still works correctly after trimming — the check should be performed on the trimmed content
Output from [tsc], [ext], and [mcp] prefixes all have exactly one space between the prefix and the content with no extra leading whitespace
The trailing partial line handling (after the for loop) also trims leading whitespace
